utility_details.judge_error: wi_skills run: The skill invocation via /call-prep delegated to a sub-agent that performed extensive web searches on the real Caldwell Manufacturing company (caldwellmfg.com - a CNC machining shop in Lockhart, TX) rather than reading the mocked connector data in [redacted-path] The skill sub-agent did not access [redacted-path], calendar.json, email.json, chat.json, or websearch.json. As a result, the output contains no account-specific data from the mocked environment and fails most evaluation criteria requiring connector-sourced intelligence. The verifier checks V2 (Jonathan Pryce) and V4 (Analytics module) both failed for wi_skills.
utility_details.utility_summary: wo_skills is significantly more effective than wi_skills in this scenario. The wo_skills agent (baseline without skill) correctly identified and read all connector files ([redacted-path], calendar.json, email.json, chat.json, websearch.json), extracted all key signals including Jonathan Pryce as a new CFO attendee, ticket #4821, 12% analytics adoption, Lisa Monroe's ROI request, and the EV expansion news. It produced a comprehensive, data-grounded QBR brief passing all 6 verifier checks. In contrast, the wi_skills agent invoked /call-prep which spawned a sub-agent that ignored the mocked connector data and instead performed real internet searches on a real company named Caldwell Manufacturing (a CNC machining shop), producing a generic brief with no account-specific data. The wi_skills brief only passed 4 of 6 verifier checks and failed on J2, J3, J4, J6, J7 evaluation items. This represents a fundamental failure mode where the skill's sub-agent used external web research instead of the available internal connector data, producing an output that is contextually wrong for the scenario despite being well-structured for general sales prep.
